repo.description: The most widely used Python to C compiler
repo.name: cython

body:
Closes #7258

The main reason to do this is to avoid going through a C varargs call which typically isn't hugely efficient. We mostly use these in module setup code (where performance isn't a huge issue) so possibly this isn't a huge worry. But there's probably little reason not to.

A secondary reason is that it avoids an MSVC+ARM compiler bug when creating large tuples and list.

Note that I haven't replaced PyTuple_Pack outside of module setup code.

(There's maybe also a question if this makes the Limited API slightly worse in that `PyTuple_Pack` can use the quick internal `SET_ITEM` macros, while our version has to use the slower checked functions)

comment_data.body:
If the intention is to reduce code overhead in init functions, then we'd better not request inlining.
(EDIT: Although those are specifically marked to produce "small code", so the end result should be the same. Still, in the current usages, we don' _intend_ them to be inlined.)

commit.message:
Replace tuple and list pack with "FromArray"

Closes #7258

The main reason to do this is to avoid going through a C varargs
call which typically isn't hugely efficient. We mostly use these
in module setup code (where performance isn't a huge issue) so
possibly this isn't a huge worry. But there's probably little
reason not to.

A secondary reason is that it avoids an MSVC+ARM compiler bug
when creating large tuples and list.

Note that I haven't replaced PyTuple_Pack outside of module
setup code.
